title: Login Monitoring & Security Incident Procedures — Brute Force to Clinical SSO

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
spl:
index=ad sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode="4625" earliest=-24h
| eval user=TargetUserName
| eval src=src_ip
| stats count values(EventCode) as codes dc(ComputerName) as dc_used by user, src
| where count>=15 AND dc_used>=3
| lookup hipaa_clinical_apps.csv src OUTPUT app_name
| sort - count
value: Surfaces credential stuffing and password spray against Hyperspace/SSO endpoints so incidents are detected and handled under HIPAA security incident procedures.
implementation: (1) Baseline legitimate auth patterns during go-live; (2) integrate with ES notable or SOAR playbooks; (3) document IR steps for suspected credential compromise affecting ePHI.
visualization: Choropleth (src geo if iplocation enabled), Table (user, src, count), Single value (distinct locked accounts).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
